title: Brain Fog and You: 8 Herbal Tinctures for a Clear Mind

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Bilobalides are good for brain fog because they help improve memory and cognitive function by protecting brain cells from damage and promoting the growth of new neurons, reducing the feeling of mental fogginess and confusion.

Quercetin is good for brain fog because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the brain, improve blood flow, and boost the production of neurotransmitters, such as dopamine and acetylcholine, which are essential for clear thinking and focus.

Kaempferol is good for brain fog because it has antioxidant properties that help protect brain cells from damage, reduce inflammation, and improve blood flow to the brain, leading to clearer thinking and increased mental clarity.

Isorhapontigenin is good for brain fog because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can cause mental fatigue and brain fog.
Ginkgolide B is good for brain fog because it can help improve blood flow to the brain and reduce inflammation, allowing for clearer thinking and reduced mental fogginess.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures may help with brain fog because it improves blood flow to the brain. Brain fog is often caused by a lack of oxygen and nutrients reaching the brain. Ginkgo biloba tinctures contain compounds that help make blood vessels wider, allowing more oxygen and nutrients to reach the brain. This improved blood flow may help increase focus, memory, and mental clarity. The tincture may also have antioxidant properties, which help protect the brain from damage caused by free radicals. This damage can lead to feelings of fatigue, confusion, and mental fogginess. Some people also use Ginkgo biloba tinctures to help reduce inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to brain fog. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, Ginkgo biloba tinctures may help alleviate symptoms of brain fog and improve overall brain function.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Acacia concinna herbal tincture that helps with brain fog, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Acacia concinna leaves or roots, a clean glass jar with a lid, vodka or another high-proof spirit, and a coffee filter. First, prepare the plant material by cleaning and drying it completely. Then, fill the glass jar about one-third full with the dried Acacia concinna leaves or roots. Next, pour vodka or the chosen spirit over the plant material, making sure that the leaves or roots are completely covered. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the mixture infuse. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Finally, transfer the tincture to a dropper bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, take a few drops under the tongue as needed, ideally when experiencing brain fog or other cognitive issues.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for brain fog

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Bacosides are good for brain fog because they can cross the blood-brain barrier and stimulate the growth of nerve cells, which helps to improve memory, concentration, and cognitive function.
Bacopa flavonoids are good for brain fog because they help improve blood flow to the brain, boost memory, and enhance cognitive function by acting as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents.

Bacoside A is good for brain fog because it improves memory and cognitive function by increasing the production of a protein called neurotrophic factor, which helps to protect and repair brain cells, thereby reducing feelings of confusion and mental fogginess.

remedies.remedy_desc: Bacopa monnieri tincture is a natural supplement that may help with brain fog. Brain fog is a feeling of being tired and confused in the head, making it hard to think clearly. The tincture is made from the Bacopa monnieri plant, which has been used in traditional Indian medicine for centuries. It contains compounds that may help improve memory and concentration. These compounds, called bacosides, may work by protecting brain cells from damage and promoting the growth of new cells. When taken regularly, Bacopa monnieri tincture may help reduce brain fog by improving mental clarity and focus. It may also help with learning and memory, making it easier to remember things and stay focused on tasks. Some people find that Bacopa monnieri tincture helps them feel more alert and awake, especially in the morning. This can be especially helpful for people who struggle with fatigue or exhaustion.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Acacia concinna herbal tincture that helps with brain fog, you'll need dried Acacia concinna leaves or roots. Start by measuring out one part of the dried herb to two parts of a neutral solvent like vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. For example, if you're using 100 grams of dried herb, use 200 grams of vodka. Next, combine the herb and solvent in the jar and seal it tightly. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the herb infuse into the solvent. This process is called maceration. After the desired infusion time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Acacia concinna tincture is now ready to use as directed - typically by taking a few drops under the tongue or mixing it with water.
remedies.image_alt: Bacopa monnieri herbal tinctures for brain fog

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols are good for brain fog because they help improve blood flow to the brain, reduce inflammation, and boost antioxidant levels, which can clear mental haze and enhance focus and concentration.
Saponins may help reduce brain fog by increasing blood flow to the brain, which can improve concentration and memory.

Ginsenosides are good for brain fog because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and improve blood flow to the brain, which can help clear mental fog and increase focus and concentration.
Ginsenoside Rg1 is good for brain fog because it helps increase blood flow to the brain, boosts cognitive function, and reduces inflammation that can cause mental confusion and fogginess.
Ginsenoside Rb1 is good for brain fog because it helps increase blood flow to the brain and protects brain cells from damage, which can improve concentration and reduce feelings of confusion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng tinctures may help with brain fog because it has properties that can improve mental clarity and focus. Ginseng is an herb that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to boost energy and mental performance. The active compounds in ginseng, such as ginsenosides, can help increase blood flow to the brain and reduce inflammation, which may contribute to brain fog. This increased blood flow can bring more oxygen and nutrients to brain cells, allowing them to function more efficiently. Additionally, ginseng has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, which can help protect brain cells from damage and promote healthy brain function. As a result, ginseng tinctures may help alleviate symptoms of brain fog, such as difficulty concentrating and memory problems, allowing for improved mental clarity and focus.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Acacia concinna herbal tinctures that help with brain fog, you'll need dried Acacia concinna leaves, a clean glass jar with a lid, vodka or a high-proof spirit, and a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. Start by measuring one part of dried Acacia concinna leaves to two parts of vodka in the glass jar. Stir well to combine, then seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the jar every day. This process is called maceration, and it allows the leaves to infuse their active properties into the vodka. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, discarding the solids. This tincture can be taken sublingually (under the tongue) or added to water, and it's recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed. The Acacia concinna tincture may help improve mental clarity and reduce brain fog due to its potential adaptogenic and cognitive-boosting properties.
remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal tinctures for brain fog

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Madecassoside is good for brain fog because it helps improve blood flow to the brain, reduces inflammation, and protects neurons from damage, thereby clearing mental confusion and improving focus and concentration.
Asiaticoside is good for brain fog because it helps increase blood flow to the brain, which can improve concentration and reduce mental confusion, allowing for clearer thinking and focus.

Flavonoids are good for brain fog because they improve blood flow to the brain, boost antioxidant levels, and reduce inflammation, which helps to clear mental confusion and increase focus and concentration.

Triterpenoids are good for brain fog because they help to improve blood circulation to the brain, reduce inflammation, and increase the production of antioxidants, which can help to clear brain fog and improve mental clarity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica tincture is often used to help with brain fog. Brain fog is when you feel tired, confused, and have trouble focusing. This can be caused by many things, like stress, lack of sleep, or poor diet. Centella asiatica contains chemicals that can help calm the mind and body. It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling in the brain and help it work better. Additionally, Centella asiatica has antioxidants that protect the brain cells from damage. This can help improve memory and concentration. The tincture can also help with stress and anxiety, which are common causes of brain fog. When taken regularly, Centella asiatica tincture may help reduce brain fog symptoms by promoting relaxation, improving focus, and enhancing cognitive function.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Acacia concinna herbal tincture, start by gathering fresh or dried Acacia concinna leaves and roots. You will need a clean glass jar with a lid, vodka or another high-proof spirit, and a dark glass bottle for storing the tincture. Begin by cleaning the jar and your hands thoroughly, then add one part of the Acacia concinna leaves and roots to the jar, and two parts of the vodka. Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the herbs release their active ingredients into the vodka. After the steeping period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be used to help alleviate brain fog by adding a few drops to water or tea, and it is best consumed in small amounts, ideally 20-30 drops, three to four times a day.
remedies.image_alt: Centella asiatica herbal tinctures for brain fog

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosavin is good for brain fog because it helps to improve blood flow to the brain, increase oxygen delivery, and reduce inflammation, all of which can help to clear mental fogginess and improve focus and concentration.
Salidroside is good for brain fog because it helps increase blood flow to the brain, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can cause mental confusion and memory loss.

Tyrosol is good for brain fog because it has antioxidant properties that help protect the brain from damage and inflammation, and also improves blood flow to the brain, which can help reduce fatigue and mental confusion.
Flavonoids are good for brain fog because they have antioxidant properties that help protect the brain from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and improving cognitive function.

Polyphenols are good for brain fog because they have antioxidant properties that help protect brain cells from damage, promote healthy blood flow to the brain, and support the production of neurotransmitters that improve focus and concentration.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rhodiola rosea tincture is an herbal supplement made from the root of the rhodiola rosea plant. It's been used for centuries to help people feel more alert and focused. The tincture is believed to work by reducing stress and anxiety that can cause brain fog. When we're stressed, our bodies release chemicals like cortisol, which can make it hard to think clearly. Rhodiola rosea tincture may help to balance these chemicals, making it easier to concentrate and feel more alert. This herb may also improve mood and reduce fatigue, which are common symptoms of brain fog. Additionally, rhodiola rosea tincture has been shown to improve memory and reaction time, which can be beneficial for people who experience brain fog due to a lack of sleep or a busy lifestyle. Some people find that taking rhodiola rosea tincture regularly helps to improve their mental clarity and reduce feelings of mental fogginess.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Acacia concinna herbal tincture that helps with brain fog, start by gathering clean glass jars, a sharp knife, a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, a strainer, and a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. Begin by drying the Acacia concinna leaves in a low-temperature oven (around 150°F) for a few hours or by air-drying them for a week. Once dry, chop the leaves into small pieces and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Combine the powder with a neutral solvent like vodka or glycerin in a 1:5 ratio (one part herb to five parts solvent) and mix well. Place the mixture in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the solvent extract the herb's active properties. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids. Finally, transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid, label it, and store it in a cool, dark place. The Acacia concinna tincture is now ready to use, and you can take a few drops sublingually (under the tongue) to help alleviate brain fog.
remedies.image_alt: Rhodiola rosea herbal tinctures for brain fog

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Schisandrins are good for brain fog because they have antioxidant properties that help protect brain cells from damage and inflammation, which can improve focus, memory, and mental clarity.

Schisandrin B is good for brain fog because it has antioxidant properties that help protect brain cells from damage and inflammation, reducing symptoms of brain fog such as confusion and difficulty concentrating.
Glycosides are good for brain fog because they contain a compound called saponin that helps to improve blood flow to the brain, boost memory, and reduce inflammation that can cause mental fogginess.
Flavonoids are good for brain fog because they have antioxidant properties that help protect the brain from damage and inflammation, promoting clear thinking and improved memory.

Polyphenols are good for brain fog because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect brain cells from damage and promote a healthy blood flow to the brain, reducing mental fogginess and improving concentration and memory.

remedies.remedy_desc: Schisandra chinensis tinctures have been used for centuries in traditional medicine to help with brain fog. Brain fog is a common symptom where people feel tired, confused, and have difficulty concentrating. Schisandra chinensis is a plant that grows in Asia and contains powerful antioxidants and adaptogens. Adaptogens are substances that help our body adapt to stress and change.  When we're stressed or tired, our brain can feel cloudy and foggy. This is because our body is producing more stress hormones, like cortisol, which can interfere with our brain's ability to think clearly. Schisandra chinensis tinctures contain compounds that can help reduce cortisol levels and promote the production of neurotransmitters like dopamine and serotonin, which are important for focus and mental clarity. The antioxidants in Schisandra chinensis tinctures can also help protect our brain from damage caused by free radicals. Free radicals are unstable molecules that can cause cell damage and contribute to brain fog. By reducing the amount of free radicals in our brain, Schisandra chinensis tinctures can help promote a clear and focused mind.  In traditional Chinese medicine, Schisandra chinensis is considered a 'qi tonic' that helps to nourish and balance the body's energy. This can help to improve mental clarity and reduce brain fog. Many people who take Schisandra chinensis tinctures report feeling more alert, focused, and able to think more clearly.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Acacia concinna tincture for brain fog relief, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Acacia concinna leaves and roots, a clean glass jar with a lid, vodka or another high-proof liquor, and a dropper bottle. Begin by measuring 1 part of the dried plant material and 2 parts of the vodka in the glass jar. Ensure the plant material is completely covered by the liquid. Next, seal the jar and place it in a cool, dark place, such as a cupboard or pantry. Allow the mixture to steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the liquid extract the beneficial compounds from the plant. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into the dropper bottle, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and take 10-15 drops sublingually (under the tongue) as needed to help alleviate brain fog and improve cognitive function.
remedies.image_alt: Schisandra chinensis herbal tinctures for brain fog

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Withanolides are good for brain fog because they help reduce stress and inflammation in the brain, promoting clarity and focus by increasing the production of brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF), a protein that supports healthy brain function.
Saponins are good for brain fog because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress in the brain, which is often associated with foggy thinking and mental clarity issues.

Alkaloids have been found to be beneficial in reducing brain fog due to their ability to increase the production of neurotransmitters, such as dopamine and serotonin, which help to improve concentration and mental clarity.
Flavonoids are good for brain fog because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to protect and repair brain cells, improving mental clarity and focus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera tincture is an herbal supplement that may help reduce brain fog. Brain fog is a feeling of confusion, tiredness, and difficulty concentrating. It's caused by stress, lack of sleep, and other factors that affect the brain. Withania somnifera is an adaptogenic herb, meaning it helps the body adjust to stress. It contains compounds like withanolides, which may reduce inflammation in the brain. This can help improve memory, focus, and mental clarity. The tincture may also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to brain fog. When taken regularly, Withania somnifera tincture may improve sleep quality, which is essential for a clear and focused mind. It may also boost the production of neurotransmitters like dopamine and serotonin, which help regulate mood and cognitive function. As a result, the tincture may help alleviate brain fog, allowing people to feel more alert, focused, and mentally refreshed.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Acacia concinna herbal tincture that helps with brain fog, start by gathering high-quality dried Acacia concinna leaves and roots, which are rich in glycosides that can help improve cognitive function. First, combine 1 part of the dried leaves and roots with 2 parts of a high-proof spirit, such as vodka or rum, in a clean glass jar. Make sure to label the jar with the date, herb used, and ratio of herb to spirit. Next, seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the glycosides from the herb infuse into the spirit. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Finally, add a small amount of glycerin or water to the tincture to help preserve it and make it easier to take, and store it in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, simply take a few drops in water or another liquid as needed, ideally 2-3 times a day, to help alleviate brain fog and improve mental clarity.
remedies.image_alt: Withania somnifera herbal tinctures for brain fog

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for brain fog because it helps reduce inflammation in the brain, improves blood flow to the brain, and protects brain cells from damage, all of which can help clear up mental confusion and improve focus.
Kaempferol is good for brain fog because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the brain, which can cause brain fog and improve cognitive function.

Tannins are good for brain fog because they have antioxidant properties that help protect brain cells from damage and inflammation, reducing symptoms of brain fog such as fatigue and difficulty concentrating.

Isoquercetin is good for brain fog because it helps improve memory and focus by acting as an antioxidant that protects brain cells from damage and inflammation, and also supports the health of blood vessels in the brain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Acacia concinna tincture is a natural remedy that may help with brain fog. Brain fog is a feeling of confusion and disorientation, often caused by stress, fatigue, and poor sleep. The tincture is made from the leaves of the Acacia concinna tree, which is native to India and other parts of Asia. It contains a compound called glycosides, which are powerful antioxidants that can help protect the brain from damage and inflammation. This can lead to improved memory, concentration, and mental clarity. Additionally, Acacia concinna tincture may help to regulate the body's stress response, reducing feelings of anxiety and fatigue that can contribute to brain fog. Some people also use it to improve their mood and reduce symptoms of depression. By taking Acacia concinna tincture, you may be able to increase blood flow to the brain, which can help to reduce symptoms of brain fog and promote a sense of mental well-being.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Acacia concinna herbal tinctures for brain fog, you'll need dried Acacia concinna roots or leaves. First, prepare a clean glass jar by washing it with soap and water, and letting it dry completely. Fill the jar about 2/3 with the dried Acacia concinna herb, and cover it with a high-proof vodka or rum - 80% or higher. Make sure the herb is completely covered with the liquid. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This process allows the herb's active ingredients to infuse into the liquid. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, take 20-30 drops in water or another liquid as needed, ideally before meals. Keep in mind that you should consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or take medications.
remedies.image_alt: Acacia concinna herbal tinctures for brain fog

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a natural and effective way to combat brain fog, a common symptom characterized by mental fogginess, difficulty concentrating, and memory problems. Brain fog can significantly impact daily life, making it hard to focus on work, relationships, or even everyday tasks. One of the main reasons to fix brain fog is that it can lead to decreased productivity, strained relationships, and a lower quality of life.  Two herbs that have shown promise in improving cognitive function and reducing brain fog are Ginkgo biloba and Bacopa monnieri. Ginkgo biloba is known for its ability to improve blood flow to the brain, which can help increase oxygenation and reduce inflammation. This can lead to improved memory, concentration, and mental clarity. Bacopa monnieri, on the other hand, has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to enhance memory and cognitive function. It's believed to work by improving the growth and health of neurons in the brain, which can help improve memory and reduce brain fog.  Using herbal tinctures containing these herbs can be a simple and natural way to address brain fog. By incorporating Ginkgo biloba and Bacopa monnieri into your daily routine, you may experience improvements in cognitive function, memory, and overall mental clarity. This can have a significant impact on your daily life, allowing you to focus more easily, complete tasks more efficiently, and enjoy a higher quality of life. Additionally, using herbal tinctures can be a more natural and gentle approach compared to prescription medications, which can have negative side effects.
supplementary_best: A combination of Bacopa monnieri, Ginkgo biloba, and Rhodiola rosea tinctures may help remedy brain fog. Bacopa monnieri, an Ayurvedic herb, improves memory and cognitive function by enhancing the growth of neurons in the brain. Ginkgo biloba, known for its blood flow-promoting properties, may enhance oxygen delivery to brain cells, reducing mental fatigue and improving focus. Rhodiola rosea, an adaptogenic herb, helps the brain adapt to stress and promotes mental clarity. This combination may be effective because Bacopa monnieri and Ginkgo biloba work synergistically to support brain health, while Rhodiola rosea helps mitigate the negative effects of stress on brain function.
supplementary_avoid: If you have brain fog, there are some herbal tinctures you should avoid.  Valerian root tincture can worsen brain fog due to its sedative properties. It can make you feel drowsy and less alert.  Kava tincture is also something to be cautious about. It can cause drowsiness and may worsen anxiety, which can further contribute to brain fog.  Skullcap tincture can also have a negative effect on brain function. It can slow down your thoughts and make you feel less alert.  Passionflower tincture is another herb to be careful with. It can cause drowsiness and may worsen anxiety, which can worsen brain fog.  Ashwagandha tincture should be used with caution if you have brain fog. While it can help with stress, high doses can cause drowsiness and worsen brain fog.  Licorice root tincture can also worsen brain fog due to its high sugar content. It can cause a blood sugar spike, which can lead to fatigue and brain fog.  Korean ginseng tincture can worsen brain fog in some people. While it can help with energy, it can cause insomnia and restlessness, which can worsen brain fog.  These tinctures can worsen brain fog due to their sedative, sleep-inducing, or blood sugar-altering properties.
supplementary_other_remedies: Brain fog can be caused by a lack of sleep, poor nutrition, and stress.  One natural remedy that can help is omega-3 fatty acids, which are found in foods like salmon and walnuts.  These fatty acids help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to brain fog.  They also support the health of brain cells and improve communication between them.  This can help improve focus, memory, and mental clarity.  Another remedy is vitamin D, which is important for nerve health and can help reduce symptoms of depression, which can contribute to brain fog.  Some people find that drinking enough water can also help with brain fog.  Dehydration can cause a lack of focus and mental clarity, and even mild dehydration can impair cognitive function.  When the body is well-hydrated, it can function at its best, and the brain can stay focused and alert.  Another natural remedy is ashwagandha, which is an herb that's often used in Ayurvedic medicine.  It's been shown to help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to brain fog.  Ashwagandha also helps regulate the body's response to stress, which can help improve mental clarity and focus.  Adaptogenic herbs like rhodiola and ginseng can also help with brain fog.  These herbs help the body adapt to stress and promote a sense of calm and well-being.  They can help improve mental clarity, memory, and focus, and may even help reduce symptoms of depression and anxiety.  These herbs work by regulating the body's response to stress, which can help improve cognitive function and reduce brain fog.  They can also help improve sleep quality, which is important for brain health and can help reduce symptoms of brain fog.  Some people find that changing their diet can also help with brain fog.  Eating a diet rich in fruits, vegetables, and whole grains can provide the body with the nutrients it needs to function at its best.  A diet high in sugar and processed foods can cause inflammation and impair cognitive function, which can contribute to brain fog.  Eating foods that are rich in antioxidants, like berries and leafy greens, can help reduce inflammation and improve cognitive function.  Other remedies that may help with brain fog include acupuncture and meditation.  Acupuncture involves the insertion of small needles into specific points on the body to stimulate healing and relaxation.  It can help reduce stress and anxiety, and may even help improve cognitive function.  Meditation involves focusing the mind and reducing stress and anxiety.  It can help improve mental clarity, focus, and memory, and may even help reduce symptoms of depression and anxiety.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience brain fog often have other health issues. One common condition is fatigue, which can be caused by low iron levels. The herb ashwagandha can help increase iron levels and reduce fatigue. Ashwagandha also reduces stress and anxiety, which can contribute to brain fog.  Depression is another condition that often occurs with brain fog. St. John's Wort, a herbal supplement, can help reduce symptoms of depression by increasing serotonin levels in the brain.  Another condition associated with brain fog is insomnia. Valerian root is a natural sleep aid that can help improve sleep quality and duration. This can help reduce brain fog caused by lack of sleep.  Hormonal imbalances, particularly low thyroid hormones, can also cause brain fog. Bladderwrack, a seaweed-based herb, can help regulate thyroid hormones and improve brain function.  Inflammation in the body can also cause brain fog. Turmeric, which contains a compound called curcumin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and improve brain function.  Lastly, people with brain fog often have digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Peppermint oil can help soothe digestive issues and reduce symptoms of IBS.
